lesson.big_idea: Water moves through evaporation, condensation, and precipitation in a continuous cycle. Each stage changes water's form without destroying it.

sections.title: Opening
sections.body: Follow a drop of water around the Earth.

sections.title: Concept
sections.body: The water cycle is a continuous process.

sections.title: Understanding
sections.body: Heat turns liquid water into vapour; cooling forms clouds.

sections.title: Reflect
sections.body: Which stage can you observe outdoors?

lesson.svg_diagram: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="220" height="90" viewBox="0 0 220 90"><rect width="220" height="90" rx="14" fill="#ecfdf5"/><text x="110" y="50" text-anchor="middle">Evaporation → Condensation → Rain</text></svg>
